title: Twentieth Anniversary Tote
gist: A product page for n+1's twentieth-anniversary merchandise, showing an independent magazine monetizing brand identity through domestically manufactured tote bags, prints, and bundled box sets keyed to cinephile cultural signifiers.

insights.claim: n+1 markets a twentieth-anniversary tote at $25 alongside an $80 print, offering an automatic 20% bundle discount when both are purchased together, illustrating cross-sell and bundling tactics for a small publisher's merch line.
insights.speaker: N+1 Magazine
insights.so_what: Shows how independent media diversify revenue beyond subscriptions via branded merchandise and bundled upsells.
insights.locator: The tote matches our Twentieth Anniversary Print

insights.claim: The merchandise is manufactured domestically at Enviro Tote in New Hampshire, signaling a 'Made in America' positioning aimed at the magazine's values-aligned readership.
insights.speaker: N+1 Magazine
insights.so_what: Domestic-manufacturing branding can command premium pricing with a politically conscious consumer base.
insights.quote: Made in America at Enviro Tote in New Hampshire.

insights.claim: The tote's design uses lettering inspired by Godard's late-1960s title cards, tying the product's appeal to cinephile cultural signifiers rather than the magazine's masthead alone.
insights.speaker: N+1 Magazine
insights.so_what: Illustrates niche cultural branding as a merchandising strategy for targeting a specific taste community.
insights.quote: featuring lettering inspired by Godard’s late ’60s title cards
insights.locator: To mark the occasion we’re introducing
